The Notice of Intention to Move for a New Trial was untimely under Code of Civil Procedure section 659. Moreover, Defendants fail to show that they were prejudicially surprised or that the court erred in granting summary judgment in favor of the plaintiff. The court finds that the jurisdiction argument only applies to the new trial ground and there is no equitable ground, either extrinsic fraud or extrinsic mistake, to support vacating the judgment and no basis under CCP 473.
